title: Prevalence of severe food insecurity in the population (%)
definition: The percentage of people in the population who live in households classified as severely food insecure. A household is classified as severely food insecure when at least one adult in the household has reported to have been exposed, at times during the year, to several of the most severe experiences described in the FIES questions, such as to have been forced to reduce the quantity of the food, to have skipped meals, having gone hungry, or having to go for a whole day without eating because of a lack of money or other resources.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata
source.sourceOrganization: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO)

editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include South Sudan, Malawi, Haiti, while the lightest include Armenia, Belarus, Cyprus; that contrast is the fastest way to read the severe food insecurity pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Severe food insecurity gives advanced players a harder version of the food-security signal.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Moderate or severe food insecurity can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Undernourishment can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Infant mortality can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Expert difficulty: the map can be mistaken for adjacent indicators unless players probe a high, low, and middle country.
regionalSignals: Agriculture maps reward separating productivity, land share, and workforce dependence.
Compare farming belts with arid, forested, and highly urban economies.

reviewNotes: Batch 3 QA: strong but close to moderate-or-severe food insecurity.
Expert-only so advanced players can explore the sharper food-security signal without padding ordinary Practice.
